narration: There's a hidden enemy inside your retirement account — invisible, automatic, and it costs more than a house. It's called the expense ratio. You've probably never looked at it.
visual: Dark background. Vault SVG with spinning dial. 'YOUR RETIREMENT HAS A SECRET ENEMY' springs in. 'THE 1% FEE' subtitle fades in green. Tagline 'Invisible. Automatic. Costs more than a house.' fades below.

narration: Every mutual fund charges a management fee called an expense ratio. The average actively managed fund charges about one percent per year. Sounds tiny, right?
visual: Light background. Fund prospectus document slides in from left. 'EXPENSE RATIO: 1.00%' highlighted in green. 'MANAGEMENT FEE' label fades in. Expanding green pill shows '~1% PER YEAR'. Quote 'Sounds tiny, right?' fades below.

narration: Here's where it gets ugly. On a hundred-thousand-dollar investment growing at seven percent, that one percent fee silently eats your compounding growth — year after year.
visual: Dark background. Bar grows upward as year counter ticks from 0 to 40. Red-to-orange gradient bar beside live counter showing cumulative fees lost up to $199K. Note 'Compound growth you'll never see.' fades in.

narration: Over forty years, the difference between a one-percent fund and a zero-point-zero-four-percent index fund is three hundred and twenty thousand dollars. That's not a typo.
visual: Light background. Two bars grow side by side — red $1.03M bar for 1% fund and taller green $1.35M bar for index fund. '$320,000 DIFFERENCE' badge springs in. Bottom text confirms the amount.

narration: Ten trillion dollars — with a T — is sitting in high-fee actively managed funds right now. The fund managers pocket billions every year. Are you getting any of that?
visual: Dark background. Six stacks of illustrated cash bills appear one by one. '$10T' counter animates upward in green. 'TRILLION' label below. Punchline 'Fund managers get rich. Do you?' fades in.

narration: Pull up your 401k right now. Tap each fund and find the expense ratio. If it's above zero-point-one percent, swap it for the index fund version. Keep that three hundred and twenty thousand.
visual: Dark background. Phone SVG with 401k app screen. Three numbered checklist items appear: 1 Open your 401k app, 2 Find each fund's expense ratio, 3 Above 0.1%? Switch. Green 'KEEP $320,000' badge springs in at bottom.
